---
title: Font fallback does not crash LuaLaTeX
# luaotfload appends a ";-fallback" feature to every font name passed to
# add_fallback. A font name only terminates at ":" "(" or "/" in luaotfload's
# request grammar, so without a trailing ":" the appended ";-fallback" is
# swallowed into the font name, the lookup fails, and LuaLaTeX crashes
# (luaotfload-fallback.lua: attempt to index a nil value). The generated
# LaTeX must emit each fallback font name colon-terminated, for every entry
# produced by the template's for-loop over multiple fallbacks.
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: lualatex
    keep-tex: true
mainfont: "Latin Modern Roman"
monofont: "Latin Modern Mono"
monofontfallback:
  - "Latin Modern Roman"
  - "Latin Modern Sans"
_quarto:
  tests:
    pdf:
      ensureLatexFileRegexMatches:
        -
          - 'luaotfload\.add_fallback\("monofontfallback"'
          - '"Latin Modern Roman:"'
          - '"Latin Modern Sans:"'
---

A paragraph with some `inline code` to exercise the monospace font and its
fallbacks.
